title: [Sentry ERROR] TypeError: Failed to resolve module specifier '@sentry/react'
body:
**Sentry Error Report** (auto-created by pipeline)

**Error:** TypeError: Failed to resolve module specifier '@sentry/react'
**Location:** ?(<anonymous>)
**Level:** error
**Events:** 1 | **Users affected:** 0
**First seen:** 2026-03-24T19:20:40.419000Z
**Last seen:** 2026-03-24T19:20:40.419000Z
**Sentry link:** https://self-6iw.sentry.io/issues/7361077397/
**Sentry ID:** 7361077397

---
_This issue was auto-created from a Sentry error report. Fix the root cause and the Sentry issue will be auto-resolved on deploy._
enhanced_description: The app fails to resolve the '@sentry/react' module specifier at runtime. This is likely a build/import configuration issue — either Sentry is not installed as a dependency, or the import path is wrong for the bundler (Vite). Affected area: Sentry integration / app entry point. Expected: Sentry initializes without module resolution errors. Acceptance criteria: App loads without '@sentry/react' resolution error; Sentry captures errors correctly.
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/index.tsx (line 55)
Approach: Changed dynamic import from bare specifier "@sentry/react" to "@/lib/sentry" (which re-exports Sentry). Bare npm specifiers in dynamic imports can fail to resolve at runtime in the browser. Using the centralized module ensures Vite bundles it correctly.
Trade-offs: None — this is strictly a bug fix using the existing abstraction.

title: [Sentry ERROR] Error: Rendered more hooks than during the previous render.
body:
**Sentry Error Report** (auto-created by pipeline)

**Error:** Error: Rendered more hooks than during the previous render.
**Location:** _e(babybloom/assets/vendor-wGySg1uH)
**Level:** error
**Events:** 1 | **Users affected:** 0
**First seen:** 2026-03-24T19:36:11.637000Z
**Last seen:** 2026-03-24T19:36:11.637000Z
**Sentry link:** https://self-6iw.sentry.io/issues/7361110696/
**Sentry ID:** 7361110696

---
_This issue was auto-created from a Sentry error report. Fix the root cause and the Sentry issue will be auto-resolved on deploy._
enhanced_description: React threw 'Rendered more hooks than during the previous render' — a hooks ordering violation. This happens when a component conditionally calls hooks (e.g., early return before a hook, or hook inside if/loop). Location: vendor bundle, so the error surfaces in a component during render. Affected area: one or more React components in src/ with conditional hook calls. Expected: All hooks are called unconditionally in every render path. Acceptance criteria: No hooks-ordering errors in Sentry after fix.
implementation_notes:
Already fixed in commit e151577 ("Fix React error #310").
The hooks violation was in HomeTab.tsx where useEffects and useMemo were after the if (!birth) early return.
That commit moved all hooks before the early return. The Sentry error at 19:36 UTC was captured ~2 min
before the fix deployed at 19:38 UTC — it is a residual error from the pre-fix build.
No additional code changes needed.

title: [Sentry ERROR] TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating 'H.type.startsWith')
body:
**Sentry Error Report** (auto-created by pipeline)

**Error:** TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating 'H.type.startsWith')
**Location:** ?(babybloom/assets/index-DJLw0WkL)
**Level:** error
**Events:** 1 | **Users affected:** 0
**First seen:** 2026-03-24T19:57:10.367000Z
**Last seen:** 2026-03-24T19:57:10.367000Z
**Sentry link:** https://self-6iw.sentry.io/issues/7361153877/
**Sentry ID:** 7361153877

---
_This issue was auto-created from a Sentry error report. Fix the root cause and the Sentry issue will be auto-resolved on deploy._
enhanced_description: TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating 'H.type.startsWith') in the main app bundle (index-DJLw0WkL). A variable H (likely a feed/activity entry) has an undefined 'type' property, and code calls .startsWith() on it without a null check. This is likely the same root cause as #77 (different bundle hash from a different build). Affected area: likely the activity/feed filtering or rendering logic in src/. Expected: Code handles entries with missing/undefined 'type' gracefully. Acceptance criteria: No 'H.type.startsWith' TypeError in Sentry; entries with missing type are filtered or handled.
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/features/reports/PediatrReport.tsx (line 75)
Approach: Added optional chaining (e.type?.startsWith) to guard against feed entries with undefined type.
The minified 'H.type.startsWith' in Sentry maps to this single .type.startsWith('Breast') call.
Issues #77 and #78 are the same bug from different bundle hashes (different deploys).
